ranked.name: Permit activity monitor
ranked.niche: Monitor municipal permit filings and surface high-intent construction / renovation signals by geography and job type.
intent_signals: Contractors, subs, and suppliers already pay for lead lists and permit feeds.
Permit activity is a recurring operational signal, not a novelty search.
ranked.pain_point: Builders, subs, suppliers, and local service operators do not have a simple way to track permit activity without messy portals or expensive data vendors.
current_solutions: sales reps pulling leads by hand
ranked.proposed_solution: A focused permit intelligence tool with alerts, filters, geography slices, and simple lead views.
ranked.access: Public permit portals, open-data catalogs, ESRI endpoints, municipal records.
ranked.mvp_scope: One geography + one permit type + alerts + simple dashboard/export.
ranked.why_this_could_win: Strong operational pain, repeat usage, local wedges, and clear B2B buyers.

ranked.name: Local business website grader
ranked.niche: Find local businesses with weak websites and generate actionable audit briefs tied to outreach or productized fixes.
intent_signals: Agencies and consultants already sell audits and local website fixes.
Business owners care when the output is specific and tied to revenue leakage.
ranked.pain_point: Local businesses often have weak websites, missing trust signals, poor CTA structure, and no tracking, but nobody surfaces that clearly at scale.
ranked.proposed_solution: A grader that scores local business websites and outputs audit-ready briefs.
ranked.access: Google Maps listings, business websites, public web pages.
ranked.mvp_scope: One niche + one city + basic site scoring + PDF/brief export.
ranked.why_this_could_win: Fast path to service revenue or outbound tooling.
risks: quality matters more than automation volume

ranked.name: Recall alert intelligence
ranked.niche: Track vehicle recall changes and alert owners, shoppers, and small operators when relevant recalls appear.
intent_signals: People actively search for recall status by vehicle.
Monitoring creates recurring value beyond one-time lookup.
ranked.pain_point: Recall information exists, but it is fragmented, clunky, and not designed for ongoing monitoring.
ranked.proposed_solution: Free recall lookup plus paid multi-vehicle monitoring and alerting.
ranked.access: NHTSA data, manufacturer guidance, structured vehicle metadata.
ranked.mvp_scope: Lookup pages + email capture + single-vehicle alerts.
ranked.why_this_could_win: Clear wedge, clear search intent, and already partially built through RecallRadar.
risks: need enough coverage to feel credible

ranked.name: Code enforcement / violation monitor
ranked.niche: Track city code enforcement, violations, and nuisance cases to surface property distress and service opportunities.
intent_signals: Investors, service providers, and local operators care about property distress signals.
Distress data can be used for lead generation and market intelligence.
ranked.pain_point: Property distress signals are scattered across local systems and are hard to watch consistently.
ranked.proposed_solution: Local distress monitor with alerts, watchlists, and territory views.
ranked.access: Municipal code-enforcement records, open-data endpoints, public case logs.
ranked.mvp_scope: One city + one violation class + alert feed.
ranked.why_this_could_win: Good signal quality and strong niche use cases in property and local services.
risks: compliance / optics depending on use case

ranked.name: Estate / public notice intelligence
ranked.niche: Monitor probate, estate, lien, and public notice signals that can feed niche services, research, or acquisition workflows.
intent_signals: Operators already mine public notices manually.
High-friction data creates room for a cleaner product.
ranked.pain_point: Public notices contain valuable signals, but the data is ugly, dispersed, and painful to normalize.
ranked.proposed_solution: A notice monitor that structures and alerts on chosen signal types.
ranked.access: County/court/public notice systems, PDFs, local publication feeds.
ranked.mvp_scope: One notice type + one geography + alert summaries.
ranked.why_this_could_win: Messy high-friction data with clear operator value.
risks: some niches may be ethically or reputationally touchy
